name: Multi-Environment Workspace Management
description: Use Terraform workspaces to manage multiple environments like dev, staging, and production from a single configuration.

steps.title: List existing workspaces
steps.description: View all available workspaces. The asterisk marks the currently selected workspace.

steps.title: Create workspaces for each environment
steps.description: Create separate workspaces for dev, staging, and production environments.
steps.command: terraform workspace new dev && terraform workspace new staging && terraform workspace new prod
steps.tip: Each workspace maintains its own independent state file, so resources are fully isolated.

steps.title: Switch between workspaces
steps.description: Select the workspace you want to operate in before running plan or apply.

steps.title: Use workspace name in configuration
steps.description: Reference terraform.workspace to parameterize resources per environment.

steps.title: Use workspace-specific variable files
steps.description: Load environment-specific variables automatically based on the active workspace.
steps.tip: Create envs/dev.tfvars, envs/staging.tfvars, and envs/prod.tfvars with environment-specific values.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

# Terraform Workspace Setup Script
# Creates and configures workspaces for multi-environment management

ENVIRONMENTS=("dev" "staging" "prod")

echo "==> Current workspaces:"
terraform workspace list

for ENV in "${ENVIRONMENTS[@]}"; do
  if terraform workspace list | grep -q "\b${ENV}\b"; then
    echo "==> Workspace '${ENV}' already exists, skipping."
  else
    echo "==> Creating workspace '${ENV}'..."
    terraform workspace new "${ENV}"
  fi
done

echo ""
echo "==> All workspaces:"
terraform workspace list

echo ""
echo "==> Switching to dev workspace..."
terraform workspace select dev

echo "==> Initializing in dev workspace..."
terraform init

echo ""
echo "Done. Use 'terraform workspace select <env>' to switch environments."

faq.question: Are workspaces suitable for large-scale environment isolation?
faq.answer: Workspaces work well for small to medium setups. For large teams, separate root modules per environment or tools like Terragrunt offer stronger isolation and independent state management.

faq.question: Where does each workspace store its state?
faq.answer: With S3 backend, each workspace creates a separate state file under the env:/ prefix in the same bucket, like env:/staging/terraform.tfstate.
